title: Chinese national arrested over attempt to smuggle 2,000 queen ants from Kenya

paragraphs.english: A Chinese national has been arrested in Kenya's main airport accused of attempting to smuggle more than 2,000 queen garden ants out of the country.
paragraphs.pidgin: A Chinese national don dey arrested for Kenya im main airport accused of attempting to smuggle more than 2,000 queen garden ants out of di country.

paragraphs.english: Zhang Kequn was intercepted during a security check at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA) in the capital Nairobi after authorities discovered a large consignment of live ants in his luggage bound for China.
paragraphs.pidgin: Zhang Kequn bin intercepted during a security check for Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA) for di capital Nairobi after authorities discovered a big consignment of live ants for im luggage bound for China.

paragraphs.english: He has yet to respond to the accusation but investigators said in court that he was linked to an ant-trafficking network that was broken up in Kenya last year.
paragraphs.pidgin: Im get yet to respond to di accusation but investigators tok for court dat im bin dey linked to an ant-trafficking network dat bin broken up for Kenya last year.

paragraphs.english: The ants are protected by international bio-diversity treaties and their trade is highly regulated.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di ants dey protected by international bio-diversity treaties and dem trade na highly regulated.

paragraphs.english: Last year, the Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS) warned of a growing demand for garden ants – scientifically known as Messor cephalotes - in Europe and Asia, where collectors keep them as pets.
paragraphs.pidgin: Last year, di Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS) warned of a growing demand for garden ants – scientifically known as Messor cephalotes - for Europe and Asia, where collectors keep dem as pets.

paragraphs.english: A state prosecutor told the court on Wednesday that Zhang had packed some ants in test tubes, while others were concealed in tissue paper rolls hidden in his luggage.
paragraphs.pidgin: A state prosecutor tell di court on Wednesday dat Zhang bin $2 some ants for test tubes, while others bin dey concealed for tissue paper rolls hidden for im luggage.

paragraphs.english: "Within his personal luggage there was found 1,948 garden ants packed in specialised test tubes," prosecutor Allen Mulama told the court.
paragraphs.pidgin: "Inside im personal luggage e dey find 1,948 garden ants packed for specialised test tubes," prosecutor Allen Mulama tell di court.

paragraphs.english: "A further 300 live ants were recovered concealed in three rolls of tissue paper within the luggage," he added.
paragraphs.pidgin: "A further 300 live ants bin dey recovered concealed for three rolls of tissue paper inside di luggage," im added.

paragraphs.english: The prosecutor asked the court to allow the suspect's electronic devices - phone and laptop - to be forensically examined.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di prosecutor ask di court to allow di suspect im electronic devices - phone and laptop - to be forensically examined.

paragraphs.english: Duncan Juma, a senior KWS official, told the BBC that more arrests were expected as investigators widen their probe into other Kenyan towns where ant harvesting was suspected to be ongoing.
paragraphs.pidgin: Duncan Juma, a senior KWS official, tell di BBC dat more arrests bin dey expected as investigators widen dem probe into other Kenyan towns where ant harvesting bin suspected to be ongoing.

paragraphs.english: Last May, a Kenyan court sentenced four men to one year in prison or a fine of $7,700 (£5,800) for trying to smuggle thousands of live queen ants out of the country, in a first-of-its kind case.
paragraphs.pidgin: Last May, a Kenyan court sentenced four men to one year for prison or a fine of $7,700 (£5,800) for trying to smuggle thousands of live queen ants out of di country, for a first-of-im kind case.

paragraphs.english: The four suspects – two Belgians, a Vietnamese and a Kenyan - had pleaded guilty to the charges after their arrest in what the KWS described as "a co-ordinated, intelligence-led operation".
paragraphs.pidgin: Di four suspects – two Belgians, a Vietnamese and a Kenyan - bin $2 guilty to di charges after dem arrest for wetin di KWS described as "a co-ordinated, intelligence-led operation".

paragraphs.english: The Belgians told the court that they were collecting the highly sought-after ants as a hobby and didn't think it was illegal.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di Belgians tell di court dat dem bin dey collecte di highly sought-after ants as a hobby and no think e bin na illegal.

paragraphs.english: Investigators now say Zhang was the mastermind behind this trafficking ring but apparently escaped Kenya last year using a different passport.
paragraphs.pidgin: Investigators now tok Zhang bin di mastermind behind dis trafficking ring but apparently escaped Kenya last year using a different passport.

paragraphs.english: On Wednesday, the court allowed prosecutors to detain him for five days to enable detectives to conduct further investigations.
paragraphs.pidgin: On Wednesday, di court allowed prosecutors to detain im for five days to enable detectives to conduct further investigations.

paragraphs.english: The KWS, which is more used to protecting larger creatures, such as lions and elephants, described last year's ruling as a "landmark case".
paragraphs.pidgin: Di KWS, which na more use to protecting larger creatures, such as lions and elephants, described last year im ruling as a "landmark case".

paragraphs.english: The ants seized last year were giant African harvester ants, which KWS said were ecologically important, noting that their removal from the ecosystem could disrupt soil health and biodiversity.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di ants seized last year bin dey giant African harvester ants, which KWS tok bin dey ecologically important, noting dat dem removal from di ecosystem fit disrupt soil health and biodiversity.

paragraphs.english: It is believed that the intended destinations were the exotic pet markets in Europe and Asia.
paragraphs.pidgin: e na believe tok di intended destinations bin dey di exotic pet markets for Europe and Asia.

paragraphs.english: Go to BBCAfrica.com for more news from the African continent.
paragraphs.pidgin: Go to BBCAfrica.com for more news from di African continent.

paragraphs.pidgin: Follow us on Twitter @BBCAfrica, on Facebook for BBC Africa or on Instagram for bbcafrica
